data.title: S&P 500 futures slip, Nasdaq rises amid mixed trade. Dow closes above 46,000, U.S. inflation at 2.9% in August.
data.description: U.S. stock futures were mostly declining as Dow closed above 46,000 for the first time despite gloomy economic data. Inflation accelerated to 2.9% in August, with weekly jobless claims spiking to 263,000. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.04% and the two-year bond was at 3.56%. The FedWatch tool shows a 100% chance of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates for the upcoming decision. Dow Jones futures were down 0.17%, S&P 500 down 0.14%, Nasdaq 100 down 0.06%, and Russell 2000 down 0.45%. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust was down 0.035% at $657.40, while the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF advanced 0.13% to $584.82. Most sectors closed positively with consumer discretionary, health care, and materials stocks recording gains, while energy stocks closed lower. Major indices hit record highs as investors anticipate falling interest rates.

data.title: Seminar in Houston on Oct 14-15, 2025, to explore U.S. electric power industry, renewable energy, ISO markets, and power transactions.
data.description: A new training program on "Today's U.S. Electric Power Industry, Renewable Energy, ISO Markets, and Power Transactions" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. Taking place in Houston, United States on October 14th-15th, 2025, this seminar aims to provide insights into the U.S. electric power industry and various mechanisms of power trading. It offers a detailed understanding of the structure, operations, and present condition of the industry. This program is designed for individuals looking to enhance their knowledge in this sector.

data.title: Heartland Express Inc. to pay $0.02 per share dividend on Oct. 3rd, totaling $1.6 million for 78 million shares.
data.description: Heartland Express, Inc. (Nasdaq: HTLD) based in North Liberty, Iowa, declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.02 per share on September 12, 2025. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on October 3, 2025, to shareholders of record as of September 23, 2025. The company anticipates a total payment of $1.6 million on its approximately seventy-eight million common stock shares. This marks the eighty-ninth consecutive quarterly cash dividend for the company, bringing the total cash dividends paid to $559.9 million, including four special dividends, since the initiation of the program in the third quarter of 2003.

data.title: NEO Battery receives $4.5 million order for silicon battery solutions from drone manufacturer in Asia. Development agreement secured.
data.description: In Toronto on September 12, 2025, NEO Battery Materials Ltd. announced the receipt of its first multi-year purchase order worth $4.5 million CAD and the establishment of a Joint Development Agreement with an Asian manufacturer focused on AI-powered mission flight control systems for drones and UAVs. NEO, known for its low-cost silicon-enhanced battery materials, aims to improve battery performance, enabling longer run times and faster charging. The partnership will see NEO's innovative battery products integrated to provide increased capacities, reinforced power output, and reduced weight for enhanced flight duration, payload capacity, and overall mission efficiency.
